text:
--u3/rZRmxL6MmkK24
 Content-Type: text/plain; charset=us-ascii
 Content-Disposition: inline
 Content-Transfer-Encoding: quoted-printable
 
 Hi Kurt,
 
 Kurt Jaeger wrote:
 > The pkg-plist, from what I understand, contains an unresolved merge
 > conflict -- and some additional pkg-plist-files which are not really
 > required ?
 
 Indeed, this does not look good. Could it be that you manually edited
 this pkg-plist file and it broke when you updated the ports tree?
 If you are using svn, you can try to remove all pkg-plist* files in this
 directory and run 'svn up' to re-fetch them.
 The current version should not have this issue - as listed on
 http://svnweb.freebsd.org/ports/head/graphics/webp/=20
 
 If you did not modify this file yourself, then can you explain how you
 update the ports tree on this system. Do you use subversion?
 
 
 Regards,
 Johan
 
 --u3/rZRmxL6MmkK24
